rxn_class_summary: Photoredox/nickel dual-catalyzed three-component 1,2-dialkylation of unactivated alkenes with alkyl halides and hypervalent iodine(III) reagents via bimolecular homolytic substitution (SH2).

key_intermediates.name: carboxyl radical from I-O homolysis of iodine(III) reagent (fragments to CO2 + alkyl radical)
nucleophilic primary alkyl radical (i, e.g. methyl radical from PhI(OAc)2)
electrophilic alkyl radical ii (e.g. •CH2CF3 from ICH2CF3 via XAT)
sterically congested secondary C-radical iii from addition of ii to alkene
Tp*Ni(III)(Me)(R) complex (v) formed by radical capture of i by Ni(II)

electron_flow: Visible-light excitation of 4-CzIPN generates a triplet that triplet-triplet energy-transfers to the iodine(III) reagent, homolyzing I-O to release a nucleophilic 1° alkyl radical (i, +CO2, +PhI); i abstracts iodine from the electrophilic alkyl iodide (XAT) to give an electrophilic alkyl radical ii, which adds regioselectively to the unactivated alkene to give congested radical iii; meanwhile i is selectively captured by Tp*Ni(II) to form Tp*Ni(III)(alkyl) v, which undergoes outer-sphere SH2 with iii to forge the second C(sp3)-C(sp3) bond and release Ni(I)/Ni(II) for turnover.
rate_determining_step: Outer-sphere bimolecular homolytic substitution (SH2) of secondary C-radical iii at the Tp*Ni(III)-alkyl species v to forge the second C(sp3)-C(sp3) bond (proposed key selectivity-determining step).

probe_reagents.name: exogenous haloarene (4-bromoiodoarene)
probe_reagents.observation: Haloarene fully recovered and reaction unaffected (84% 4a), arguing against low-valent Ni(0)/Ni(I) that would oxidatively insert into Ar-X.

probe_reagents.name: methyl iodide (in place of PhI(OAc)2)
probe_reagents.observation: No dialkylation detected, showing the second C(sp3)-C(sp3) bond does not form via Ni insertion into Me-I and that the Me source must come from the iodine(III) reagent.

probe_reagents.name: dicumyl peroxide (alternative methyl radical source)
probe_reagents.observation: Affords 4a in 14% yield, supporting that photolytically generated Me• can engage in XAT with ICH2CF3.

probe_reagents.name: PhI(O2CEt)2 with ICH2CN
probe_reagents.observation: Gives product 5m (45%) along with EtI (110%), confirming chemoselective XAT in which the ethyl radical from the I(III) reagent abstracts iodine from the electrophilic alkyl iodide.

notes: Authors explicitly disfavor a low-valent Ni(0)/Ni(I) oxidative addition pathway based on the haloarene control. Polarity matching (nucleophilic 1° radical preferentially abstracts I from electrophilic EWG-CH2-I) and steric differentiation by Tp*Ni (preferring the unhindered 1° alkyl radical) are invoked to rationalize chemoselectivity. Ring-opening of α-pinene/caryophyllene oxide substrates serves as an implicit radical clock supporting cyclobutylcarbinyl-radical intermediacy after initial electrophilic radical addition. No KIE, EPR, DFT, or CV reported in the main text.
